rationale: Both candidates correctly validate the unet addition as safe and independently rediscover the same underlying theme as dsocek's ground-truth comment (hardcoded component lists should derive from a single source of truth), neither hitting his exact packed_modules_mapping suggestion. X adds a genuinely distinct second finding (text-encoder LoRA silently no-op'ing) that Y misses entirely, but Y backs its overlapping two comments with much heavier grounding — explicit consumer sweep, a concrete missed case (MagiHuman's 'sr_dit'), and repo-wide grep evidence — giving it a slight precision/rigor edge despite near-identical comment content. Actionability is comparable in both, with concrete file/line suggestions and proposed code changes.
